title: athlete-guardian — TenancyHook + lifecycle hooks
description: Cross-module hook implementations. AthleteGuardian does not register a bootstrapper. Registers three cascade hooks: user-archive prevention, tenant-erasure cascade, and auto-primary election on primary removal.

hooks.name: PreventUserArchiveWithActiveGuardianship
hooks.purpose: Refuse the User soft-delete when the User holds an ACTIVE guardianship for a MINOR athlete (age_years < config.athlete.minor_min_age) AND is the sole remaining guardian for that minor. Guardian handoff must complete first — remove the guardian only after adding a replacement. For guardians of adult athletes, the archive is allowed (adult athletes don't strictly need a guardian on record).
hooks.returns: USER_HOLDS_MINOR_GUARDIANSHIP (409) with the athlete_id in the error payload
hooks.note: Priority 47 — runs AFTER PreventUserArchiveWithActiveAthlete (priority 45) and PreventUserArchiveWithActiveStaff (priority 40). The staff / athlete records win when multiple would fire — those are more common day-to-day; the guardian hook catches the residual case.

hooks.name: PurgeAthleteGuardianDataForErasedTenant
hooks.purpose: Cascade delete via FK. Writes compliance audit trail entry + emits count metrics per verification status. No dedicated audit-history table for guardians (their state history is captured by the normal audits table via Auditable trait).
hooks.compliance: GDPR Art. 17 — right to erasure. Guardian rows cascade with the tenant.

hooks.name: ReleasePrimaryGuardianSlotOnGuardianRemoved
hooks.purpose: When the removed guardian was is_primary=true, auto-elect a new primary from the earliest-added remaining active + verified guardian. Fires PrimaryGuardianChanged with the from/to pair. When no remaining verified guardian exists, elects the earliest-added pending guardian (with a warning metric — the athlete temporarily has an unverified primary until the verify job runs).
hooks.note: Priority 20 — runs BEFORE notification listeners so the primary-changed notification is dispatched with the resolved new primary.

no_bootstrapper.reason: AthleteGuardian does not need a tenant-boot hydration path — every row loads on demand. The PrimaryGuardianResolver + ConsentDelegationChain bindings cache lazily on first query.
